skill.name: Agent Component
skill.description: Batteries-included agent component for React and Next.js with runtime, tools, streaming, approvals, and widgets built in
skill.summary: A complete AI agent UI component with human-in-the-loop approvals, client-side tools, and real-time streaming
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill containing only SKILL.md with installation instructions and usage examples. No executable code, scripts, or network operations were detected. The skill provides guidance for integrating a React agent component from ui.inference.sh.

content.user_title: Build AI Agent Interfaces with React
content.value_statement: Building AI chat interfaces requires complex state management, streaming, and tool handling. This skill provides a ready-to-use agent component with all features built in.
actual_capabilities: Provides drop-in React agent component with streaming token output
Implements human-in-the-loop approval flows for tool execution
Supports client-side tools that run directly in the browser
Handles tool lifecycle states including pending, progress, approval, and results
Renders declarative JSON UI widgets from agent responses
Integrates with shadcn UI components for consistent styling
limitations: Requires Next.js or React project with TypeScript support
Needs valid INFERENCE_API_KEY environment variable for backend proxy
Client-side tools are limited to browser-safe operations only
Proxy route setup required for secure API communication

use_cases.title: SaaS Copilot Integration
use_cases.description: Add an AI copilot to your SaaS application with minimal setup. The component handles streaming, tool calls, and user approvals out of the box.
use_cases.target_user: SaaS developers building in-app AI assistants

use_cases.title: Internal Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Create internal tools where AI agents can execute actions with human oversight. Approval flows ensure sensitive operations are reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Customer Support Chatbot
use_cases.description: Deploy a support chatbot that can fetch data, fill forms, and escalate to humans when needed. The widget system provides rich interactive responses.
use_cases.target_user: Support teams implementing AI-powered help desks

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple agent component with Claude Haiku that responds to user questions. Use the default configuration with streaming enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic chat functionality

prompt_templates.title: Agent with Custom System Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure an agent component with a custom system prompt for code review. The agent should analyze pull request diffs and suggest improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Specializing the agent for a specific domain task

prompt_templates.title: Agent with Client-Side Form Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up an agent with client-side tools for form interaction. The agent should use scan_ui to read form fields and fill_field to populate data based on user requests.
prompt_templates.scenario: Enabling the agent to interact with page elements

prompt_templates.title: Agent with Approval Workflows
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure an agent that requires human approval before executing sensitive operations like database writes or external API calls. Set up approval handlers that show context before confirmation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing safety controls for high-risk actions

output_examples.input: Install and configure the agent component with basic settings
output_examples.output: A fully functional chat interface appears with message input, conversation history, and streaming responses from Claude. The component handles connection state, errors, and reconnection automatically.

output_examples.input: Add a tool that requires approval before sending emails
output_examples.output: When the agent attempts to send an email, a approval widget appears showing the recipient, subject, and message content. The user can approve or reject the action before execution.

best_practices: Always use a backend proxy route to protect your API key from client-side exposure
Implement approval flows for any actions that modify data or trigger external systems
Use client-side tools only for browser-safe operations like reading form values or scrolling
anti_patterns: Never expose INFERENCE_API_KEY directly in client-side code or commit it to version control
Do not use the agent component without a proxy route as this exposes credentials
Avoid granting approval permissions for sensitive operations without proper user confirmation UI

faq.question: What is the Agent Component?
faq.answer: The Agent Component is a pre-built React component that provides a complete AI chat interface with streaming, tool execution, and human-in-the-loop approvals. It integrates with the Inference.sh platform and works with Claude, Codex, and other AI models.

faq.question: Do I need a backend to use this component?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to set up a proxy route in your Next.js application to forward requests to the Inference API. This protects your API key and handles authentication securely on the server side.

faq.question: Can the agent execute code on my server?
faq.answer: No, the agent component itself does not execute server-side code. It provides a UI for AI conversations. Any tool execution happens through the Inference platform with proper authentication and approval flows.

faq.question: What are client-side tools?
faq.answer: Client-side tools are functions that run in the browser, such as reading form values, filling fields, or scrolling the page. They are declared in your React component and the AI can call them with user approval.

faq.question: How does the approval workflow work?
faq.answer: When the agent wants to execute a tool marked as requiring approval, the component shows an approval widget with the tool name and arguments. The user must explicitly approve before the tool executes.

faq.question: Is this component compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, the Agent Component works with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex through the Inference platform. You configure which model to use in the agentConfig prop when rendering the component.
